{"text":"Create a UML Use Case Diagram titled “LiCEnSURE System”.

System Boundary:
- Draw a system boundary labeled “LiCEnSURE System”.

Actors:
1. Chairman / Dean (positioned on the left side of the system boundary)
2. Staff (positioned on the right side of the system boundary)

Use Cases for Chairman / Dean:
- Login Authentication
- Manage User Accounts and Roles
- View Prediction Dashboard
- View Student Prediction Details
- View Model Performance Summary
- Generate Predictive Reports
- View Audit Logs
- Logout

Use Cases for Staff:
- Login Authentication
- Import CSV Files
- Validate Uploaded Data
- Clean and Encode Data
- Run Prediction
- View Upload and Processing Summary
- Manage Students Records
- Logout

Include Relationships:
- Import CSV Files «include» Validate Uploaded Data
- Validate Uploaded Data «include» Clean and Encode Data
- Run Prediction «include» Validate Uploaded Data

Layout and Style Requirements:
- Chairman / Dean is connected only to strategic and monitoring use cases.
- Staff is connected only to operational and data-processing use cases.
- Place data-processing use cases near the bottom center of the system boundary.
- Use dashed arrows for «include» relationships.
- Use solid association lines between actors and use cases.
- Apply standard UML notation.
- Keep the diagram clean, balanced, and suitable for academic proposal and thesis presentation.
","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Create a high-fidelity, responsive web-based system prototype named “LiCEnSURE – Licensure Exam Predictive System”.

SYSTEM PURPOSE
Design a decision-support system for predicting Civil Engineering licensure exam success using machine learning. The system must support early identification of at-risk students and provide institutional insights for academic leaders.

BRANDING AND THEME
Use USTP official branding:
• Primary color: Navy Blue (#0B2C5D)
• Secondary color: Gold / Yellow (#F2B705)
• Accent color: White and Light Gray backgrounds
• Typography: Clean, academic, professional (e.g., Inter or Roboto)
• Style: Minimal, modern, trustworthy, university system aesthetic

LOGO HANDLING
• Landing page must include a logo placeholder component
• Logo must be easily replaceable (drag-and-drop or swap instance)
• Show default text logo: “LiCEnSURE | USTP”
• Indicate “Logo can be replaced” in the component description

USER ROLES
1. Chairman / Dean (Strategic, Read-Only + Reports)
2. Staff (Operational, Data Processing)

LANDING PAGE
• System name and tagline: “Data-Driven Prediction for Licensure Exam Success”
• Login button
• Replaceable logo at top left
• Background with subtle academic / analytics pattern
• Footer with USTP attribution

AUTHENTICATION
• Login screen with role-based access
• Simple, clean form (email, password)
• No student self-login

DASHBOARD – CHAIRMAN / DEAN
Create a dashboard focused on decision-making:
• Prediction summary cards:
  – Total students analyzed
  – Predicted pass rate
  – At-risk students count
• Interactive charts:
  – Risk distribution
  – Prediction confidence overview
• Navigation menu:
  – Dashboard
  – Student Predictions
  – Model Performance Summary
  – Predictive Reports
  – Audit Logs
  – User Management
• Read-only data views
• No upload or prediction execution controls

STUDENT PREDICTION DETAILS (CHAIRMAN / DEAN)
• Table of students with:
  – Student ID
  – Risk classification
  – Prediction result
• Click-to-view detailed prediction profile
• Emphasize interpretation, not technical controls

MODEL PERFORMANCE SUMMARY
• Accuracy, precision, recall, validation metrics
• Simple visual indicators (progress bars, charts)
• Plain-language explanation of model reliability

PREDICTIVE REPORTS
• Report generation screen
• Filters by cohort, year level, risk category
• Download and print-ready report layout

AUDIT LOGS
• System activity log table
• Shows user, action, date/time
• Read-only view for Chairman / Dean

DASHBOARD – STAFF
Create an operational dashboard focused on data handling:
• Navigation menu:
  – Import CSV Files
  – Validate Uploaded Data
  – Clean and Encode Data
  – Run Prediction
  – Upload & Processing Summary
  – Manage Student Records
• Step-by-step workflow layout

DATA IMPORT AND PROCESSING
• CSV upload screen
• Data validation status indicators
• Cleaning and encoding progress display
• Clear confirmation before running prediction
• Visual pipeline showing:
  Import CSV → Validate Data → Clean & Encode → Run Prediction

UPLOAD & PROCESSING SUMMARY
• Log of recent uploads
• Processing status (success, failed, pending)
• Timestamp and action history

STUDENT RECORD MANAGEMENT (STAFF)
• Table view for student records
• Edit and update data fields
• No access to decision dashboards

SECURITY AND ACCESS
• Enforce strict role-based UI separation
• Chairman/Dean cannot upload or process data
• Staff cannot access strategic dashboards or reports

INTERACTION STYLE
• Professional academic system
• Clear hierarchy and spacing
• Accessible color contrast
• Dashboard-first layout
• University-grade UI suitable for thesis defense

OUTPUT
Generate:
• Desktop and tablet views
• Clear page flow between screens
• Component-based design for scalability
","providerOptions":{}}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building a high-fidelity web prototype for "LiCEnSURE – Licensure Exam Predictive System," a decision-support system for predicting Civil Engineering licensure exam success using machine learning. The system uses USTP official branding (Navy Blue #0B2C5D, Gold #F2B705) and serves two distinct user roles: Chairman/Dean (strategic, read-only dashboards with prediction analytics, reports, and audit logs) and Staff (operational, data processing workflows including CSV import, validation, cleaning, and prediction execution). The design follows a clean, academic aesthetic with proper role-based access control, responsive layouts, and interactive charts. I also created a UML Use Case Diagram showing the system boundary, actors, use cases, and include relationships between data processing workflows.

## Current state
So far, we've implemented the complete system prototype including landing page, authentication, both role-based dashboards, and all functional pages for Chairman/Dean and Staff portals. I've successfully integrated my custom LiCEnSURE logo throughout all pages, replacing the placeholder text logos in the header, navigation, and login screens.
